articles.headline: US weighs resumption of military strikes against Iran
articles.summary: Aides to US President Donald Trump have drafted plans for potential military strikes on Iran to break the impasse over the Strait of Hormuz. Options include aggressive bombing of infrastructure or ground operations by Special Forces at nuclear sites. While a ceasefire remains fragile, the Pentagon is preparing for an escalation if diplomatic efforts fail to reopen the vital waterway.

articles.headline: IMD predicts prolonged heatwave and 44°C temperatures for Delhi
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has forecast a week-long heatwave for the national capital, with maximum temperatures expected to touch 44°C. Strong surface winds are predicted to prevail. The IMD is also planning to overhaul its alert system to include a percentile-based threshold, which will better capture the impact of high humidity in coastal and inland regions.

articles.headline: Oil tanker carrying Iraqi crude reaches India via Hormuz
articles.summary: The Suezmax tanker Karolos, carrying Iraqi crude loaded at Basra, reached India after successfully navigating the Strait of Hormuz. The transit occurred despite an ongoing Iranian blockade of the waterway. Vessel-tracking data shows commercial transits remain significantly below pre-war levels, as Tehran demands sovereignty over the strait as a condition for peace talks.

articles.headline: Iran to reveal new mechanism for Strait of Hormuz traffic
articles.summary: Iran’s national security panel announced that a new mechanism to manage traffic through the Strait of Hormuz along a designated route will be unveiled soon. Officials stated that only commercial vessels and parties cooperating with Tehran would benefit from the arrangement. Fees will be collected for specialized services provided to ships allowed to pass through the strategic channel.

articles.headline: Ambedkar’s Annihilation of Caste marks 90th anniversary this month
articles.summary: B.R. Ambedkar’s seminal text, Annihilation of Caste, turns 90 this month. Originally written as an undelivered speech for a 1936 conference in Lahore, the manifesto remains a foundational document for social equality in India. The text famously critiqued the Hindu social order and advocated for a society based on liberty, equality, and fraternity through the destruction of caste.

articles.headline: Karnataka forms cabinet sub-committee to study State Education Policy
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has constituted a six-member cabinet sub-committee, led by Home Minister G Parameshwara, to review the State Education Policy (SEP). The committee will study the final report submitted by the Professor Sukhdeo Thorat-led commission and recommend implementation strategies covering curriculum reforms, financial implications, and human resources.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar rejects ‘One Nation One Election’ as unworkable
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar strongly opposed the Centre’s 'One Nation One Election' initiative, calling it a politically motivated scheme that damages India’s federal structure. Speaking at a parliamentary study committee dialogue, he questioned the feasibility of synchronizing elections if a government falls mid-term and noted the state assembly had already passed a resolution against it.

articles.headline: UDF races to finalize Cabinet berths in Kerala
articles.summary: Political activity intensified in Thiruvananthapuram as UDF leaders held meetings to finalize ministerial portfolios for the new ministry headed by CM-designate V.D. Satheesan. Discussions focused on balancing regional and community interests. Senior leader Ramesh Chennithala was reportedly pacified with the offer of the Home portfolio after initial tensions over the CM post.

articles.headline: Four arrested for illegal cattle transport in Mangaluru
articles.summary: Mangaluru police arrested four individuals for illegally transporting 27 buffaloes from Haveri to Kerala in a truck. The animals were found being carried in a cruel manner during an early morning patrol on National Highway 66. The accused were booked under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and the Karnataka Prevention of Slaughter and Preservation of Cattle Act.

articles.headline: India’s fuel price hike marginal compared to global average: BJP
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu BJP defended the recent ₹3 per litre hike in petrol and diesel prices, stating that India’s prices remain under control despite global disruptions. BJP spokesperson Narayanan Thirupathy noted that the global average petrol price is ₹145 per litre and credited the government's precautionary measures for sparing the public from the full impact of the US-Iran conflict.

articles.headline: Haveri jewelry shop looted of ornaments worth ₹40 lakh
articles.summary: Six masked men broke into a jewelry shop in Haveri district, Karnataka, stealing 19kg of silver and 219 grams of gold worth over ₹40 lakh. The suspects cut open the shutters and stole a safe locker after covering three CCTV cameras. Police have registered a case and are using footage from a fourth uncovered camera to trace the suspects and the vehicle used in the theft.

articles.headline: Tipper truck kills four women MGNREGA workers in Andhra
articles.summary: A speeding tipper truck ran over a group of six women on the National Highway in Kakinada district, killing four on the spot. The victims were daily wage workers on their way to take biometric photographs for MGNREGA work. Two other women sustained serious injuries. Local authorities have directed officials to ensure better treatment for survivors and install speed breakers.

articles.headline: Elderly couple lifted by excavator during land protest in Andhra
articles.summary: An elderly couple protesting against the takeover of their land for the Bhogapuram international airport approach road were forcibly lifted in an excavator bucket and moved aside by contractors. A video of the incident in Vizianagaram district went viral, triggering local outrage. The farmers were demanding full compensation before the government proceeded with construction.

articles.headline: Investment firm owner arrested for multi-crore fraud in Belagavi
articles.summary: Police arrested Shivanand Neelannavar, owner of Shivaam Associates, for allegedly operating an unregulated investment firm and defrauding thousands of investors in Belagavi. Preliminary inquiries suggest the firm was not registered with RBI or SEBI. Authorities believe it is a multi-crore fraud and have booked the owner under the Karnataka Protection of Interest of Depositors Act.

articles.headline: Bengaluru CM orders FIRs in 2021 post-poll violence cases
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari directed police to investigate all cases of post-2021 assembly election violence and file FIRs for every incident of murder or assault previously ignored. During an administrative review in Diamond Harbour, he alleged that police previously functioned under political pressure and vowed that those guilty of terrorizing workers would be jailed.

articles.headline: ECI probes reports of foreign nationals voting in TN polls
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has launched a probe after 25 foreign nationals were arrested for allegedly casting votes in the April 23 Tamil Nadu assembly elections. The individuals, from countries like Sri Lanka and the UK, were caught with indelible ink marks at airports. The inquiry will focus on how they were registered on electoral rolls using fraudulent ID documents.

articles.headline: Manipur Deputy CMs lead negotiations in ongoing hostage crisis
articles.summary: Manipur's Deputy CMs Losii Diko and Nemcha Kipgen are working with church leaders and security forces to secure the release of 20 people abducted during Naga-Kuki clashes. While 28 hostages were recently released through exchanges, 14 Kuki-Zo and 6 Naga individuals remain captive. The crisis follows the killing of three Kuki-Zo church leaders in an ambush on Wednesday.

articles.headline: NEET aspirant dies by suicide in Rajasthan’s Sikar district
articles.summary: A 23-year-old NEET aspirant from Jhunjhunu died by suicide in Sikar, Rajasthan, days after the exam was cancelled due to a question paper leak. His father stated that the student was depressed after news of the leak emerged, despite having performed well in the test. This was the student's third attempt at the medical entrance examination.

articles.headline: Court denies bail to TCS official in POSH abetment case
articles.summary: A Nashik court denied bail to Ashwini Chainani, a TCS site head and POSH committee member, for allegedly abetting sexual harassment. The judge noted that Chainani ignored a victim's repeated oral complaints and instead blamed her, effectively endorsing a toxic workplace. Chainani was arrested on April 10 as part of an SIT probe into nine cases of harassment at the IT firm’s Nashik unit.

articles.headline: Eight Naxals arrested in Gadchiroli; ‘wanted’ list cleared
articles.summary: Gadchiroli police arrested eight Maoists from Maharashtra, Telangana, and Chhattisgarh, while five others surrendered with a total bounty of ₹38 lakh. Officials stated that following 'Operation Antim Prahar,' no Maoists remain on the official 'wanted' records. Large quantities of arms, including AK-47 rifles and ₹63.84 lakh in cash, were recovered during the intelligence-based operations.

articles.headline: Pesticide poisoning caused death of 25 vultures in Lakhimpur
articles.summary: Lab results from the IVRI confirmed that 25 vultures found dead in Uttar Pradesh’s Dudhwa buffer zone in April died from 'carbofuran' poisoning. Officials found the birds died after consuming carcasses of stray dogs that had eaten poisoned rice. The forest department plans an awareness program for villagers regarding the lethal impact of toxic pesticides on wildlife.

articles.headline: Rare twin elephant calves born in Corbett Tiger Reserve
articles.summary: An elephant gave birth to twin calves in the Corbett Tiger Reserve's Garjiya zone, a rare occurrence in the wild. Forest officials noted that twin births are uncommon due to the long 22-24 month gestation period and physical challenges for the mother. The calves were spotted feeding by a naturalist, and their arrival is seen as a sign of a healthy habitat.

articles.headline: Ebola variant with no vaccine causing DR Congo outbreak
articles.summary: A Bundibugyo strain Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has caused at least 80 deaths and 250 suspected cases. Health officials warned the strain has a high lethality rate and no existing vaccine or specific treatment, unlike the Zaire strain. One death has also been recorded in neighbouring Uganda, prompting a large-scale medical response from Doctors Without Borders.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court Struck Down Post-2010 OBC Certificates in West Bengal
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court has cancelled all OBC certificates issued in West Bengal after 2010. The court found the identification process flawed and in violation of the 1993 Act. While approximately 5 lakh people are affected by the loss of status, the court protected those who have already secured jobs or educational admissions using these certificates.

articles.headline: RBI Approves Record Dividend Transfer of 2.11 Lakh Crore to Centre
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India board has approved a record surplus transfer of Rs 2.11 lakh crore to the Union government for the fiscal year 2023-24. This amount is significantly higher than the budgeted estimate of Rs 1.02 lakh crore. The dividend boost is expected to help the government meet its fiscal deficit targets and increase capital expenditure.

articles.headline: Norway Ireland and Spain Formally Recognize Palestinian Statehood in Historic Move
articles.summary: In a coordinated announcement, Norway, Ireland, and Spain declared they will formally recognize a Palestinian state on May 28. The leaders stated that recognition is the only way to achieve a two-state solution and lasting peace. Israel reacted sharply, recalling its ambassadors from all three nations and accusing them of rewarding terrorism.

articles.headline: Pune Porsche Crash Case Police Move Court to Try Minor as Adult
articles.summary: Pune Police have approached the Juvenile Justice Board to try a 17-year-old boy involved in a fatal Porsche crash as an adult. The minor's father, a prominent realtor, was remanded to police custody for allowing his son to drive without a license and consume alcohol. The accident resulted in the deaths of two young software engineers in the Kalyani Nagar area.

articles.headline: ECI Issues Notices to BJP and Congress Presidents Over Campaign Violations
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has directed the presidents of the BJP and Congress to ensure their star campaigners do not engage in communal or divisive rhetoric. The ECI noted a decline in the quality of political discourse during the 2024 Lok Sabha elections and asked for formal responses to complaints regarding violations of the Model Code of Conduct.

articles.headline: Severe Heatwave Grips North India as Temperatures Surge Past 47 Degrees
articles.summary: Severe heatwave conditions continued across Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana, and Delhi, with temperatures reaching 47.8 degrees Celsius in Rajasthan's Sirsa. The India Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for the region, warning of continued high temperatures. Public health advisories have been issued to prevent heat-related illnesses and dehydration.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Rejects Plea to Disqualify PM Modi for Religious Speeches
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court dismissed a petition seeking the disqualification of Prime Minister Narendra Modi from contesting elections for six years. The petitioner alleged the PM had violated the Model Code of Conduct by making religious references in speeches. The court held that it could not interfere as the Election Commission was already looking into the matter.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Royals Defeat RCB to Enter IPL 2024 Qualifier Two
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals ended Royal Challengers Bengaluru's six-match winning streak with a four-wicket victory in the IPL 2024 Eliminator match. After restricting RCB to 172, RR chased down the target with one over to spare. Rajasthan will now face Sunrisers Hyderabad in Qualifier 2 for a place in the final, while RCB has been knocked out of the tournament.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Directs SpiceJet to Pay 100 Crore to Kalanithi Maran
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has directed SpiceJet and its promoter Ajay Singh to pay Rs 100 crore to former promoter Kalanithi Maran in relation to an arbitral award dispute. The court observed that the airline had failed to comply with previous payment orders. SpiceJet was warned that non-compliance could lead to the attachment of the airline's assets.

articles.headline: Adani Group to Invest Seven Billion Dollars in Power and Infrastructure
articles.summary: The Adani Group has announced plans to invest approximately $7 billion in its power and infrastructure projects over the next year. The investment will focus on expanding green energy capacity and port operations. The group aims to significantly reduce its debt-to-EBITDA ratio while continuing its aggressive expansion in the domestic infrastructure sector.

articles.headline: Equities join gold in adding stress to India's Balance of Payments
articles.summary: India's Balance of Payments (BoP) is facing pressure as gold imports and crude oil consumption rise alongside foreign direct investment repatriations and FII selling. Gold imports surged to 4.5% of GDP in FY22, while net FDI flows slowed. Experts suggest structural shifts beyond gold and petroleum are needed to address the worsening BoP situation and protect the rupee.

articles.headline: Government restricts silver bar imports following hike in import duties
articles.summary: The Indian government has restricted the import of silver bars by moving them from the 'free' to 'restricted' category. This follows a duty hike on gold and silver to 15% and platinum to 15.4%. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade issued the order to curb non-essential imports and address concerns over the widening trade deficit and rupee weakness.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi receives Chola-era artifacts returned by the Netherlands
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi received 11th-century Chola dynasty copper plates, known as the Leiden Plates, returned by the Netherlands. The artifacts were handed over during his two-day visit to the country. The return of these historical items, which India has sought since 2012, marks a significant moment in strengthening bilateral cultural ties between the two nations.

articles.headline: Delhi government reduces VAT on aviation turbine fuel to 7 percent
articles.summary: The Delhi government has reduced the value added tax (VAT) on aviation turbine fuel (ATF) from 25% to 7% for a period of six months. This decision aims to lower operating costs for airlines, which spend about 40% of their costs on fuel. The move is expected to cause a revenue loss of approximately ₹985 crore for the government but will benefit the aviation sector.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict reduces India's LPG imports by 4.3 lakh barrels
articles.summary: Conflict in West Asia has caused a significant drop in India's Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) imports, decreasing by 430,000 barrels per day. The International Energy Agency warned that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz could further disrupt supplies for the 3.4 billion people globally who rely on clean cooking fuel. India has secured alternative supplies from the US.

articles.headline: Investors seek higher yields in bonds as inflation risks persist
articles.summary: As inflation concerns rise and the RBI maintains a pause on repo rates, investors are looking towards high-yield bond options. Experts suggest State Development Loans (SDLs) which offer spreads over government securities, and floating rate bonds to hedge against future rate hikes. AAA-rated corporate bonds and select NBFC bonds are identified as attractive options for portfolios.

articles.headline: Federal Reserve leadership transition raises questions on gold and equity performance
articles.summary: With Kevin Warsh expected to take over as US Federal Reserve Chair, analysts are examining historical asset performance under previous chairs. Data shows that gold often outperforms equities when inflation remains high or volatile, as seen during the Burns and Miller eras. Investors are watching for Warsh's approach to the 2% inflation target and its impact on market sentiment.

articles.headline: Contra funds gain traction by investing in undervalued market sectors
articles.summary: Mutual fund houses are increasingly launching 'contra funds' that target stocks or sectors currently out of favor with the market. These funds, including offerings from SBI and Kotak, aim to capitalize on potential turnarounds in sentiment. While the category is small, some funds have delivered up to 22% CAGR over five years by focusing on companies with strong fundamentals.

articles.headline: Mutual funds reduce cash reserves as markets reach new highs
articles.summary: Active equity mutual funds in India have significantly reduced their cash holdings to 4.7% of assets under management, down from 6.8% the previous year. This shift indicates a willingness to deploy capital in a rising market. While cash levels remain higher in mid-cap and small-cap funds due to valuation concerns, the overall trend reflects strategic moves to capitalize on momentum.

articles.headline: SBI Mutual Fund launches Nifty G-sec Jul 2031 Index Fund
articles.summary: SBI Mutual Fund has introduced the SBI Nifty G-Sec July 2031 Index Fund, an open-ended target maturity index fund. The scheme predominantly invests in constituents of the Nifty G-Sec July 2031 Index, offering a low-cost, relatively low-risk option for investors with a matching investment horizon. The NFO closes on May 19, with a minimum subscription amount of ₹5,000.

articles.headline: Groww Mutual Fund introduces Nifty Private Bank ETF
articles.summary: Groww Mutual Fund has launched the Groww Nifty Private Bank ETF, an open-ended scheme tracking the Nifty Private Bank Index. The NFO closes on May 20, 2026. The performance of the scheme will be benchmarked against the Nifty Private Bank Index TRI. This new offering provides investors with targeted exposure to the private banking sector through an exchange-traded fund structure.

articles.headline: Godrej Agrovet stock recommended as a long-term buy on price dips
articles.summary: Godrej Agrovet's stock has corrected 35% from its peak, making it an attractive long-term investment. Supported by palm oil and animal nutrition segments, the company expects double-digit revenue growth. Analysts suggest accumulating the stock as it trades at a reasonable valuation of 17 times FY28 estimated earnings, despite recent headwinds in the crop care segment.

articles.headline: SRF stock recommended for accumulation following strong quarterly performance
articles.summary: SRF reported a 7% year-on-year revenue growth to ₹15,787 crore for FY26, with a 44% increase in PAT. The company is investing ₹2,500 crore in its chemicals business and is building a new facility for R6, a sustainable refrigerant. Analysts suggest accumulating the stock on dips, citing a recovery in specialty chemicals and strong expansion plans despite geopolitical risks.

articles.headline: Global CEOs highlight India as a key growth market in earnings calls
articles.summary: Top executives from multinational corporations including Siemens, Eutelsat, CIE Automotive, and ICL Group are increasingly focusing on India for manufacturing and expansion. Siemens cited strong supply-chain viability, while Eutelsat expanded its partnership with Tata's Nelco for satellite services. The shift reflects growing global consensus on India's role as a major manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: Derivatives data suggests bearish outlook for Bank Nifty compared to Nifty
articles.summary: Analysis of futures and options (F&O) data indicates a stronger bearish sentiment for the Bank Nifty index. While Nifty futures saw a decrease in Open Interest, Bank Nifty futures experienced a buildup in short positions. Experts suggest traders consider short positions on Bank Nifty if it falls below key support levels, as volatility and selling pressure from foreign investors continue.

articles.headline: Investing in index-weighted baskets provides better risk management in volatile markets
articles.summary: Market analysts recommend investors consider index-based derivatives or baskets of stocks during periods of high volatility. This approach helps manage systematic risk better than focusing on individual stocks. While individual stocks can provide higher returns, the weighted average of an index offers a cushion against sharp corrections in specific sectors during times of tension.

articles.headline: Bank of India announces F&O contract adjustments following dividend declaration
articles.summary: Bank of India has announced an interim dividend of ₹1.50 per share, leading to adjustments in its futures and options (F&O) contracts. The record date for the dividend is May 29, 2026. Consequently, the strike prices and market lot sizes for June and July contracts will be modified to reflect the dividend payout, as per standard exchange regulations for corporate actions.

articles.headline: Brent crude oil futures show signs of breaking out above $115
articles.summary: Brent crude oil futures provided support in early May and are showing signs of gaining traction toward a breakout above $115. Prices remain above both 21-day and 50-day moving averages. Analysts suggest that current price action maintains a positive inclination, with a high probability of further rallies if the $115 level is breached sustainably amid ongoing supply concerns.

articles.headline: Short gold and silver futures as prices break below key supports
articles.summary: Prices of precious metals dropped last week as gold and silver broke below critical support levels. Gold futures on MCX fell to ₹1,58,547 after breaching the ₹1,55,000 mark, while silver broke below ₹3,04,891. Technical analysts suggest a short trade strategy for June gold futures with a stop-loss at ₹1,65,000, as weakness in the global market is expected to persist.

articles.headline: Nifty and Sensex face resistance levels amid ongoing market volatility
articles.summary: The Nifty and Sensex have shown signs of a breakout but face significant resistance at 24,100 and 77,000 respectively. Technical analysts suggest that failure to hold these levels could lead to further corrections toward 23,000 for Nifty. Meanwhile, foreign portfolio investors have sold approximately $1.34 billion in equities recently, adding to the downward pressure on Indian markets.

articles.headline: US markets and Treasury yields remain stable despite rising dollar index
articles.summary: Major US indices including the Dow Jones and S&P 500 closed higher despite a sharp rise in the dollar index. The US 10-Year Treasury Yield rose to 4.6 percent, reflecting expectations of sustained higher interest rates. Analysts note that while US markets show resilience, a further rise in the dollar index above 99.45 could trigger a pullback in global equity markets.

articles.headline: KEI Industries stock maintains strong uptrend supported by infrastructure demand
articles.summary: The stock of KEI Industries has broken out of a consolidation phase, supported by strong infrastructure-related demand. Technical indicators suggest a fresh leg of uptrend which could lift the stock toward ₹6,150. Analysts recommend buying on dips with a support level at ₹4,800, as the company benefits from sustained government spending on power and infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs) gain momentum as a bridge to physical gold
articles.summary: Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs) are emerging as a regulated alternative to physical gold and gold ETFs. Launched by BSE and NSE, EGRs allow investors to buy gold in demat form and convert it into physical gold if desired. The system aims to provide transparency in pricing and purity. While volumes are currently low, the move is expected to integrate India's fragmented gold market.

articles.headline: Surge in courier-related scams prompts warnings for Indian e-commerce users
articles.summary: Scammers are increasingly using fake courier delivery notifications to harvest personal data and bank details from consumers. Common tactics include 'address update' requests or claims of 'illegal content' in parcels to induce panic. Experts advise users to avoid clicking links in SMS messages and to verify tracking numbers directly on official websites to prevent financial fraud.

articles.headline: Bank of Baroda launches sign language support for hearing-impaired customers
articles.summary: Bank of Baroda has introduced Indian Sign Language (ISL) support through video calls at its Contact Centre. This service allows customers with hearing or speech impairments to access banking assistance with the help of trained ISL interpreters. The initiative is part of the bank's efforts to enhance digital accessibility and provide inclusive customer service to all segments.

articles.headline: Uno Minda plans to raise ₹2,500 crore through various financial instruments
articles.summary: Auto components major Uno Minda has announced plans to raise up to ₹2,500 crore through the issuance of equity shares, convertible bonds, or other financial instruments. The board has also approved a further investment of ₹20 crore in Unominda EV Systems Private Ltd. The funds will be used to support expansion and growth in the electric vehicle components segment.

articles.headline: IDBI Bank board approves raising ₹10,000 crore through long-term bonds
articles.summary: IDBI Bank has received board approval to raise up to ₹10,000 crore through the issuance of long-term bonds in the financial year 2026-27. The funds will be raised in one or more tranches to finance infrastructure and affordable housing projects. This strategic move is intended to strengthen the bank's capital base and support its long-term lending operations.

articles.headline: State-owned NHPC reports 68 percent jump in Q4 net profit
articles.summary: NHPC reported a 68% increase in consolidated net profit to ₹1,549.42 crore for the fourth quarter, driven by higher operational revenue. For the full year FY26, the company's net profit reached ₹4,220.46 crore. NHPC also added 1,850 MW of capacity during the year, including solar and hydro projects, and announced a final dividend of ₹1.21 per share.

articles.headline: Dr Reddy's launches generic Semaglutide injection in the Canadian market
articles.summary: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories has launched a generic version of the diabetes drug Semaglutide in Canada. The injectable medication is indicated for glycemic control in adults with Type 2 diabetes. This marks a significant expansion for the company in the North American market, as it seeks to provide more affordable treatment options for chronic health conditions.

articles.headline: Tata Trusts reviews Maharashtra Charity Commissioner's order on board composition
articles.summary: Tata Trusts is reviewing a directive from the Maharashtra Charity Commissioner regarding the composition of its board of trustees. The order follows a petition alleging that some trustees have exceeded the legal tenure limits. Tata Trusts stated it is seeking legal opinions and noted that the order appeared to be issued ex parte without a proper hearing for the trust.

articles.headline: India increases export duty on petrol and aviation turbine fuel
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased export duties on petrol and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) amid rising global crude prices. The duty on petrol exports has been raised to ₹3 per litre, while the levy on ATF has been cut to ₹1.65 per litre. These adjustments are part of the government's strategy to manage domestic fuel supplies and revenue following recent international price hikes.

articles.headline: Flipkart leads Indian e-commerce market in user growth, says CLSA
articles.summary: A report by CLSA indicates that Flipkart added 26.8 million monthly active users in FY26, outpacing competitors like Amazon and Meesho. The growth is attributed to Flipkart’s strong performance in Tier-II and Tier-III cities. While Amazon added 10.6 million users, Meesho saw a decline. The report highlights Flipkart's dominant position in the evolving Indian digital retail landscape.

articles.headline: Etihad Airways launches cricket-themed promotional activities in Chennai
articles.summary: Etihad Airways, the official sponsor of Chennai Super Kings (CSK), has launched a series of fan-focused activities in Chennai. The 'Wickets of the World' experience includes themed rickshaws and cricket challenges across the city. The airline aims to strengthen its connection with Indian travelers and cricket fans through these interactive local engagements and promotional offers.

articles.headline: Brazil Foreign Minister says BRICS will reach common position on differences
articles.summary: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira stated that despite divisions over West Asia, BRICS remains a platform for dialogue. Speaking ahead of the India summit, he noted the diversity of positions is a positive aspect of the group's evolution. Vieira clarified that BRICS is not 'anti-West' but focuses on developing common policies for trade and social development among member nations.

articles.headline: Kuki-Zo groups in Manipur demand reimposition of President’s Rule
articles.summary: Thousands of Kuki-Zo people protested in Manipur’s Churachandpur and Kangpokpi districts following the killing of three church leaders. Protesters demanded the unconditional release of hostages and the creation of special security zones. A memorandum was sent to Union Home Minister Amit Shah calling for President’s Rule to ensure civilian protection in conflict-prone areas.

articles.headline: V.D. Satheesan to submit Kerala Cabinet list by Sunday evening
articles.summary: Kerala Chief Minister-designate V.D. Satheesan announced that the full Council of Ministers will be finalized by Sunday. The swearing-in ceremony is scheduled for Monday at the Central Stadium in Thiruvananthapuram. Senior leader Ramesh Chennithala is expected to join the Cabinet as Home Minister following conciliatory talks with the Congress leadership.

articles.headline: PM Modi highlights India's unprecedented transformation during visit to The Hague
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the Indian diaspora in the Netherlands, stating that India’s aspirations are no longer limited to its borders. He described India as a 'land of opportunities' undergoing a massive shift. The visit included meetings with Dutch royalty and CEOs to discuss cooperation in semiconductors, green hydrogen, and trade.

articles.headline: Delhi government to launch new warehousing and logistics hub policy
articles.summary: Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced a new Warehousing and Logistics Policy 2025 to transform Delhi into a logistics hub. Following Delhi's 'Exemplary' ranking in the national logistics index, the policy aims to reduce freight congestion and improve digital integration. Plans include dedicated freight corridors and using the metro network for light freight transport.

articles.headline: Delhi University opens registrations for two-year postgraduate courses
articles.summary: Delhi University has launched the registration process for admissions to its two-year postgraduate programs. Candidates can apply through a new portal until June 7. The system features DigiLocker-based auto-integration for candidate credentials. Admission is based on the Common University Entrance Test (CUET-PG) results for relevant subjects.

articles.headline: Delhi L-G launches drive to restore 101 water bodies
articles.summary: Lieutenant-Governor Taranjit Singh Sandhu launched a project to revive 101 water bodies in Delhi by August 30. The initiative, managed by the Delhi Development Authority, focuses on deep desilting and catchment clearance to improve groundwater recharge during the monsoon. The L-G emphasized the importance of restoring 'blue lungs' for the city's ecological resilience.

articles.headline: UP CM lays foundation for international cricket stadium in Gorakhpur
articles.summary: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath laid the foundation stone for a 30,000-capacity international cricket stadium in Gorakhpur. The project, costing ₹393 crore, will be built on 46 acres in Tal Nador. The CM stated the facility would boost sports infrastructure and generate local employment, noting that the government has already provided jobs to 550 medal-winning players.

articles.headline: Meghalaya CM calls for revival of India-Bangladesh trade ties
articles.summary: Chief Minister Conrad Sangma advocated for resetting India-Bangladesh ties through local-level dialogue and people-to-people engagement. Speaking in Delhi, he emphasized reviving historical cooperation and reopening border haats closed after recent political unrest. Sangma highlighted the importance of trade and cultural exchanges to strengthen regional stability.

articles.headline: Political parties step up vigil over electoral roll revision in Punjab
articles.summary: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann and Haryana Congress leaders have increased monitoring of the special intensive revision of electoral rolls starting June 15. Mann stated AAP volunteers would ensure no genuine voters are deleted. In Haryana, the Congress has formed a committee to prevent wrongful deletions and ensure transparency in the voter list update process.

articles.headline: AIADMK to take action against 25 MLAs supporting TVK
articles.summary: Former AIADMK Minister O.S. Manian stated that anti-defection proceedings will be initiated against 25 party MLAs for supporting the TVK despite a party whip. Manian cited a 2023 Supreme Court verdict giving the general secretary power to appoint the whip. The move follows reports of the MLAs meeting with the Chief Minister separately.

articles.headline: Maharashtra farmers still awaiting relief months after unseasonal floods
articles.summary: Farmers in Marathwada and Solapur report significant delays in receiving compensation for crop losses and topsoil damage caused by last year's floods. Despite a ₹31,628-crore relief package announced by the state, many claim insurance payouts are stuck due to discrepancies between satellite imagery and manual crop-cutting experiments. Farmers face rising debt and fertility loss.

articles.headline: TMC fact-finding teams visit West Bengal areas hit by post-poll violence
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress teams visited South 24 Parganas, Purba Medinipur, and Hooghly to document alleged attacks on party workers by BJP supporters. MP Dola Sen claimed many people were forced to flee homes and government offices were vandalized. The findings will be submitted to the Calcutta High Court, while the BJP dismissed the exercise as 'drama.'

articles.headline: Kerala UDF government faces tightrope walk over PM SHRI scheme
articles.summary: The incoming UDF government in Kerala must decide on implementing the Union government's PM SHRI scheme, which links funding to the National Education Policy. The previous LDF government faced internal friction over the deal. With nearly ₹1,100 crore in Samagra Shiksha funds currently withheld by the Centre, the UDF faces pressure to sign the agreement to secure education budget.

articles.headline: Report highlights poor political representation of Muslims in Karnataka
articles.summary: A report by the Federation of Karnataka Muslim Organisations shows that Muslim representation in the state legislature remains far below their 13% population share. In the 224-member Assembly, Muslim share is only 4.4%. The report raised concerns over low ticket allocation by political parties and called for proportional representation in elected and nominated bodies.

articles.headline: Mosque committee to challenge Bhojshala temple verdict in Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Kamal Maula Mosque Committee and the All India Muslim Personal Law Board rejected the High Court verdict on the Bhojshala dispute. Representatives termed the decision 'one-sided' and a violation of the Places of Worship Act, 1991. They argued that the ASI is acting in contravention of established rules by allowing daily Hindu worship at the disputed site.

articles.headline: NIA files chargesheet in Cambodia-linked human trafficking and cyber slavery
articles.summary: The NIA filed a chargesheet against five individuals, including kingpin Anand Kumar Singh, for luring Indian youth to Cambodia for 'cyber slavery.' Victims were allegedly forced to work for scam companies under threat of physical torture and electric shocks. The agency stated the syndicate charged up to $3,000 per person and seized victims' passports upon arrival.

articles.headline: Doctors’ front moves SC to make NTA a statutory body
articles.summary: The United Doctors Front filed a petition in the Supreme Court seeking to transition the National Testing Agency (NTA) from a registered society to a statutory body. The petition argues that the current legal status shields the NTA from direct Parliamentary oversight and CAG audits. The group claims statutory status is necessary to ensure accountability after recurring paper leaks.

articles.headline: Barn swallows in Manipur's Imphal Valley shed migratory traits
articles.summary: A study by the Wildlife Institute of India suggests that barn swallows in the Imphal Valley have become permanent residents, abandoning their traditional migratory patterns. Researchers observed active nesting year-round, possibly due to climate change and local cultural protection. The Meitei community views the birds as symbols of prosperity, ensuring nest safety in private properties.

articles.headline: West Bengal government to reverify caste certificates issued since 2011
articles.summary: The West Bengal government has ordered a reverification of approximately 1.69 crore SC, ST, and OBC certificates issued during the Trinamool regime. The move follows challenges to the authenticity of certificates, particularly those issued under the Duare Sarkar camps. Newly appointed Minister Kshudiram Tudu alleged that many ineligible people used fake certificates for benefits.

articles.headline: Centre to roll out pilot program for out-of-school teenagers
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education is launching a pilot project in 10 districts across nine states to track and enroll out-of-school children aged 14-18 in the National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS). Estimates suggest over two crore children in this age group are currently out of the school system. The program will use app-based mapping and incentive mechanisms to encourage distance learning.

articles.headline: Analysis: India’s LPG storage capacity contradicts government rolling stock claims
articles.summary: Government claims of a 45-day LPG 'rolling stock' are being questioned as India's physical storage capacity covers only 17 days of consumption. Data shows that maintaining such a buffer requires a continuous influx of shipments, with one tanker arriving daily. The government likely counts LPG currently in transit on tankers from the U.S. and West Asia as part of the total inventory.

articles.headline: CEA Nageswaran warns of arduous global financial environment
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V. Anantha Nageswaran stated that India faces a challenging global financial climate as US Treasury yields cross 5%. He noted that India's goods trade deficit remains a structural challenge, and global capital flows are becoming more selective. Nageswaran emphasized the need for rapid job creation and strategic energy reserves to maintain growth in a fragmented world.

articles.headline: IIT-M and IISc team creates carbon-free analogue of ferrocene
articles.summary: Researchers from IIT-Madras and IISc Bengaluru have synthesized a stable, carbon-free version of the famous ferrocene sandwich molecule using boron rings and osmium. The discovery, published in Science, establishes that sandwich architecture is possible in inorganic chemistry. This could lead to the development of new catalysts that remain stable at extremely high temperatures.

articles.headline: Constant mechanical force explains why heart cancer is rare
articles.summary: New research suggests that the constant compressive force of heartbeats creates a hostile environment for cancer cells. A study in the journal Science found that mechanical stress alters DNA organization in cells, activating genes that restrain division. This physical signaling helps explain why tumours rarely form in the heart compared to other organs.

articles.headline: Ancient horse-riding evidence dates back to 3500 BC in Eurasia
articles.summary: Archaeological analysis of horse skeletons at the Botai site and Yamnaya remains indicates that humans were riding horses at least 1,000 years earlier than previously believed. Physical changes in skeletons from 3200-2600 BC suggest habitual riding. This early mobility allowed for the management of large herds and long-distance migrations, reshaping Eurasia's genetic map.

articles.headline: Why Air India has cut international flight operations drastically
articles.summary: Air India announced significant schedule cuts for North America and Europe due to the West Asia conflict and the continued ban on Pakistani airspace. Avoiding conflict zones has added up to six hours to flight times, necessitating refuelling stops and increasing costs. The airline, which posted a large loss in FY26, is rationalizing its network to manage operational pressures.

articles.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
articles.summary: The Israeli military launched air strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday, shortly after both nations agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days. Israel claimed it was targeting Hezbollah positions following evacuation warnings. The strikes highlight the fragility of the truce in the region as tensions remain high following the outbreak of war in late February.

articles.headline: Tribute to master photographer Raghu Rai after his passing
articles.summary: Raghu Rai (1942-2026), the legendary Indian photojournalist, was remembered for his poetic and symmetrical documentation of India. A Padma Shri awardee and protege of Henri Cartier-Bresson, Rai captured iconic moments ranging from the Bhopal gas leak to intimate portraits of Mother Teresa and Indira Gandhi. His work remains a definitive visual record of the nation’s soul.

articles.headline: Suvendu Adhikari directs cops to accept police excess complaints
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari has ordered police stations to register fresh FIRs for police excesses, political violence, and extortion occurring over the last five years. The move follows his first administrative meeting in Diamond Harbour.

articles.headline: Cattle slaughter crackdown hits supply at lone Kolkata abattoir
articles.summary: Supply at Tangra abattoir stopped after the new BJP government tightened enforcement of slaughter rules. Only 22 of 180 cattle were cleared for slaughter as others failed to meet age or fitness certifications required by a 1950 law.

articles.headline: UP woman fights snatchers for 23 minutes before arrests
articles.summary: Anita Devi, 45, fought off two bike-borne snatchers for 23 minutes in Etah's Jaithra area. Police arrested the accused following an encounter on Saturday after CCTV footage of the struggle went viral.

articles.headline: Gadchiroli district officially declared free of Maoist insurgency
articles.summary: Maharashtra police wiped out guerrilla presence in Gadchiroli following a 100-hour coordinated action called 'Antim Prahar'. Officials reported zero active Maoists in records, meeting a deadline set by the Union Home Minister.

articles.headline: HC calls razing of AIMIM leader's Sambhajinagar properties shocking
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court expressed displeasure over the demolition of properties linked to AIMIM's Mateen Patel. The court noted the civic body acted before a 24-hour notice period expired, despite assurances that due process would be followed.

articles.headline: Army and police in custody tussle over Pulgaon shooting
articles.summary: A dispute has emerged between the Army and Wardha police over the custody of Major Manan Tiwari, who allegedly shot a junior officer. While the Army cites 'accidental discharge', police are skeptical and pushing for detailed interrogation.

articles.headline: Study shows Eastern Ghats were once fused to Antarctica
articles.summary: Geological findings in Andhra Pradesh's granulite rocks provide evidence that the Eastern Ghats and East Antarctica were part of a single mountain system before the Gondwana supercontinent splintered millions of years ago.

articles.headline: Trump suggests US may avoid military involvement in Taiwan
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump suggested Washington might not fight for Taiwan if it seeks formal independence. He warned against the move and stated a proposed $14 billion arms package remains on hold pending China's response.

articles.headline: Captagon drug used by IS seized in Delhi; Syrian arrested
articles.summary: The NCB busted an international drug network, seizing 227.7kg of Captagon worth Rs 182 crore. A Syrian national was held in India's first seizure of the drug, which is commonly used by Islamic State operatives in West Asia.

articles.headline: CBSE implements three-language rule for Class 9 students
articles.summary: Starting 2026-27, CBSE students in Class 9 must study three languages, with at least two being native Indian languages. The board has removed formal examinations for the third language, moving it to internal assessment.

articles.headline: PM Modi warns world crisis could push millions into poverty
articles.summary: Addressing the Indian diaspora in The Hague, PM Modi warned that ongoing pandemics, wars, and energy crises could undo decades of progress. He lauded India's transformation and its emergence as a tech-driven land of opportunities.

articles.headline: India restricts silver imports to monitor surging shipments
articles.summary: The government moved silver bars into the 'restricted' category, requiring a license for imports. This follows a doubling of customs duty and aims to curb surging shipments of bullion used as investment and in industry.

articles.headline: France appoints judge to lead Jamal Khashoggi murder inquiry
articles.summary: A French court deemed a human rights group's complaint admissible, leading to the appointment of a judge to investigate the killing of journalist Jamal Khashoggi. A previous complaint had been ruled inadmissible.

articles.headline: Metro passenger wins compensation after grab handle injury
articles.summary: A consumer commission ordered BMRCL to pay Rs 5,000 to a passenger after a Metro coach grab handle snapped, injuring his nose. The commission held the operator accountable for deficiency in service and lack of guidelines.

articles.headline: Man arrested for strangling lover to death in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Police arrested 27-year-old Rathan Das for allegedly murdering his 47-year-old girlfriend, Anjali Nath. The suspect reportedly strangled her near Tirupalya lake after she threatened to file a rape case against him.

articles.headline: Streetlights installed at Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout after long delay
articles.summary: Nearly 9,000 LED streetlights have been installed at the NPKL housing project after years of litigation. While 3,918 are energised, residents still face issues with water supply, road connectivity, and infrastructure theft.

articles.headline: BSWML launches DClutter app for bulky waste collection
articles.summary: Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Ltd released a mobile app allowing citizens to schedule doorstep pickups for large items like furniture and mattresses. The move aims to reduce roadside dumping of non-routine waste.

articles.headline: Proposed elevated railway terminal at Yelahanka to cost 6,000 crore
articles.summary: South Western Railway is planning India's first elevated mega coaching terminal at Yelahanka. The project, currently under survey, will include 16 platform lines across two levels to ease congestion at KSR Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Karnataka to use AI for power grid operations by 2047
articles.summary: The state's energy department plans to integrate artificial intelligence for grid management and forecast-based power governance. Digital twins will be used for simulations to improve efficiency and power banking.

articles.headline: Doctors warn high sodium in instant foods causes hypertension
articles.summary: Health experts in Bengaluru warned that instant noodles and street foods like gobi manchurian contain excessive sodium levels. High salt intake is contributing to a rise in hypertension cases among urban youth.

articles.headline: Court acquits man in 2017 Bengaluru marital abuse case
articles.summary: A city court acquitted Vikram Vincent of charges including unnatural sex and domestic violence. The judge cited a lack of medical evidence and material contradictions in the prosecution's narrative during the trial.

articles.headline: Human remains found on Mugaluru lakebed in Bengaluru Rural
articles.summary: Residents discovered a human skull and bones scattered on the Mugaluru lakebed. Police suspect the remains belong to a woman aged 25-35 and are checking missing person reports from neighboring states.

articles.headline: Muslim forums question Congress record on election promises
articles.summary: The Karnataka Federation of Muslim Organisations released an 'accountability report' claiming the Siddaramaiah government failed to fulfill key promises. Issues include action against hate speech and revoking the hijab ban.

articles.headline: One Nation One Election: Karnataka parties trade blows
articles.summary: During a JPC meeting in Bengaluru, the BJP and JD(S) backed simultaneous elections, while the Congress opposed the move. Congress leaders argued the plan weakens federal structure and marginalizes regional parties.

articles.headline: Deve Gowda alleges land mafia operating in Bidadi project
articles.summary: Former PM H.D. Deve Gowda claimed a powerful land mafia is active in Karnataka under government patronage. He wrote to Congress leadership raising concerns over the restructuring of development authorities for the Bidadi township.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Airport surpasses 400-million passenger milestone in April
articles.summary: Kempegowda International Airport reached the 400-million flyer mark since its 2008 opening. The airport handled 44.4 million passengers in the last fiscal year, fueled by a 24% surge in international traffic.

articles.headline: HC quashes lookout circular against driver based in Saudi
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside an LOC against Abdul Khadar Nihad, a driver working in Saudi Arabia. The court ruled that continuing the travel embargo after he was granted bail reduced him to economic destitution.

articles.headline: US citizen detained for illegally voting in Tamil Nadu polls
articles.summary: A 64-year-old US citizen named Kishore was detained at Chennai airport for allegedly voting in the state assembly elections. Immigration officials noticed indelible ink on his finger, leading to a handover to the crime branch.

articles.headline: Trump disclosure reveals over 3,700 trades in first quarter
articles.summary: Financial disclosures show Donald Trump or his advisers made over 3,700 trades in major companies during the first quarter. Experts noted the volume is highly unusual for a president and resembles hedge fund activity.

articles.headline: Netherlands returns Chola copper plates to India after 14 years
articles.summary: The Netherlands formally returned 11th-century Chola Dynasty copper plates to India. The artefacts, documenting agreements on Buddhist monasteries, had been held at Leiden University since the 19th century.

articles.headline: Experts: Computer-based NEET-UG welcome but more reforms needed
articles.summary: Education experts support the government's move to transition NEET-UG to a computer-based format to prevent leaks. However, they emphasize that the massive cost gap between public and private seats remains a primary driver of malpractice.

articles.headline: Kamal Haasan urges film industry to cut costs amid crisis
articles.summary: Actor Kamal Haasan called for tighter schedules and reduced luxury expenses in filmmaking to counter inflationary pressures. He emphasized that budget cuts should not affect the wages or safety of film workers.

articles.headline: Kanye West India debut concert in Delhi cancelled
articles.summary: The highly anticipated India debut of rapper Ye (Kanye West) in Delhi was cancelled due to public safety concerns. Other international acts, including Shakira and Scorpions, have also postponed or cancelled summer dates.

articles.headline: Industry faces hardware hurdles for Direct-to-Device satellite connectivity
articles.summary: Global tech giants like Apple and Google have flagged concerns to the Department of Telecommunications regarding hardware modifications required for Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite connectivity. While D2D offers potential for emergency services and rural coverage, experts note that existing handsets need significant changes. The DoT is currently consulting with stakeholders.

articles.headline: Government imposes licensing requirements on certain silver imports
articles.summary: The Indian government has restricted imports of certain silver forms, including bars and jewelry, requiring a license for items with less than 99.9% purity. The move aims to curb surging imports, which rose 157% to $411 million in April. Previously, these items were under the 'free' category. The change is intended to prevent the misuse of Free Trade Agreement (FTA) provisions.

articles.headline: Mumbai Police file new fraud case against Yes Bank founder
articles.summary: Mumbai Police have registered a fresh FIR against Yes Bank co-founder Rana Kapoor and others for an alleged ₹1,000-crore fraud. The case involves the illegal assignment and undervalued sale of mortgaged assets. This adds to Kapoor's existing legal troubles, having been released on bail just last month in multiple cases filed by the CBI and Enforcement Directorate.

articles.headline: Government to create framework to measure India's knowledge economy
articles.summary: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation is developing a framework to measure the economic contribution of India's 'knowledge capital,' including R&D, software, and intellectual property. The initiative seeks to capture the impact of digital platforms and scientific innovations on the national GDP, moving beyond traditional physical asset tracking to reflect a modern economy.

articles.headline: Indian student applications to Ivy League colleges surge despite uncertainty
articles.summary: Indian student applications to prestigious Ivy League and top-30 US universities have seen a significant rise, contrasting with a broader decline in international applications. Despite concerns over US visa policies and high tuition costs exceeding $65,000 annually, demand for elite education remains strong, particularly in fields like technology management and computer engineering.

articles.headline: Ten people remain missing after abduction by militants in Manipur
articles.summary: Ten out of thirty-eight people abducted by armed militants in Manipur remain untraceable, according to police reports. While 28 individuals were released following a security operation, the status of the remaining victims is unknown. Kuki groups have called for a total shutdown in the Kangpokpi district to protest the continued detention of the individuals.

articles.headline: PM Modi emphasizes resilient supply chains with Netherlands
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, during a meeting with the Dutch King and Queen, highlighted the importance of building a resilient and future-ready global supply chain. He noted that the current decade is facing various 'disasters of tears' and stressed that India and the Netherlands are working together in sectors like semiconductors, electronics, and green energy to ensure stability.

articles.headline: BJP plans cabinet reshuffles in Rajasthan, Haryana, and Madhya Pradesh
articles.summary: Following recent changes in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is reportedly planning cabinet reshuffles in Rajasthan, Haryana, and Madhya Pradesh. The moves are intended to balance regional and caste representations ahead of local body elections. Chief Ministers have been meeting with the central leadership in New Delhi to finalize the new appointments.

articles.headline: Manipur cabinet expansion delayed despite ongoing regional tensions
articles.summary: Three months after the formation of the new government in Manipur, a cabinet expansion remains pending. Currently, only the Chief Minister and seven ministers are in office, leaving several key portfolios vacant. The delay is attributed to the need for approval from the BJP central leadership and the complex security situation in the state, which continues to face ethnic unrest.

articles.headline: Jagan Mohan Reddy plans local outreach to revive YSRCP prospects
articles.summary: YSRCP leader Y.S. Jagan Mohan Reddy is planning a 'hyper-local' outreach program across Andhra Pradesh following his party's defeat in the recent Assembly and Lok Sabha elections. The initiative aims to reconnect with the grassroots and address local grievances at the constituency level. Reddy has instructed party workers to identify strong local candidates for future polls.

articles.headline: National Stock Exchange releases ₹72 crore in withheld trade payouts
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has released ₹72 crore in trade payouts that were previously withheld following a police complaint by an investor. The investor had alleged unauthorized equity transactions in a client demat account. The NSE's clearing corporation (NCL) acted after the initial investigation was concluded, ensuring the funds are returned to legitimate clearing members.

articles.headline: India leading global discourse on digital public infrastructure, says PK Mishra
articles.summary: PK Mishra, Principal Secretary to the Prime Minister, stated that India's digital public infrastructure architecture is shaping global thinking on inclusive finance. Highlighting the success of the UPI and JAM trinity (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile), Mishra emphasized that these systems have dismantled barriers to financial access for millions, providing a model for other nations to follow.

articles.headline: Major e-commerce platforms delist unverified herbicide following CCPA notice
articles.summary: Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho, and JioMart have delisted an unregistered agro-chemical product called 'Cyclosione Herbicide' from their platforms. The action follows a notice from the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA), which flagged the sale of unverified hazardous chemicals. The CCPA has ordered a detailed investigation into how these products were listed without approvals.

articles.headline: Amaravati's Quantum Valley aims to become India's next tech hub
articles.summary: Amaravati, the newly designated capital of Andhra Pradesh, is developing 'Quantum Valley,' a technology park intended to host advanced quantum computing systems and deep-tech startups. The project aims to attract scientists and engineers to build an ecosystem for innovation, research, and industrial collaboration. Officials envision the valley as a hub for the state's growth.

articles.headline: 2026 Cannes Film Festival highlights global conflicts through cinema
articles.summary: The 79th Cannes Film Festival has selected several films that focus on modern wars and societal struggles. Features from the Central African Republic, Yemen, and Ukraine are being showcased, reflecting contemporary reality. Directors aim to use cinema to restore dignity and provide a platform for human stories amidst political turmoil. The festival is a crucial venue for cultural expression.

articles.headline: Pandemic-born micro-entrepreneurs adapt to post-lockdown business realities
articles.summary: Six years after the COVID-19 pandemic sparked a wave of home-grown micro-enterprises, many entrepreneurs have transitioned to stable business models. Sectors like home baking, pet care, and urban gardening saw growth, but post-pandemic shifts have forced founders to differentiate and professionalize. The 'hobby economy' in India is now estimated to be worth over ₹1.5 lakh crore annually.

articles.headline: CEOs face challenges as commercial disputes turn into criminal cases
articles.summary: Business leaders in India are increasingly concerned about the 'criminalization' of commercial disputes. Many civil matters are reportedly being converted into criminal proceedings to exert pressure on corporate executives. Experts highlight that liability in India often falls personally on directors rather than just the company, leading to calls for legal reforms.

articles.headline: US and China agree to reduce trade levies after Trump visit
articles.summary: Following a two-day visit by former US President Donald Trump to Beijing, the US and China have reportedly agreed to lower certain trade levies. A joint statement indicated both nations would adopt a series of measures to reduce mutually cutting levies on products including agriculture and technology. The move signals a potential de-escalation of trade tensions between the nations.

articles.headline: Trump claims China agreed to help reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Donald Trump has claimed that Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. While Beijing has not officially confirmed this, the statement suggests a potential shift in Middle Eastern geopolitical dynamics. The US has been seeking international support to ensure free passage through the strategic waterway amidst ongoing tensions.

articles.headline: European nations in talks with Iran for safe passage in Hormuz
articles.summary: Several European countries are reportedly in negotiations with Iran to ensure the safe passage of their commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. According to Iranian state television, these talks aim to establish a framework for maritime security and avoid further disruptions in the strategic shipping lane. The negotiations come amidst heightened regional tensions.

articles.headline: Hong Kong teahouse hosts 'dim sum raves' to attract younger patrons
articles.summary: A century-old teahouse in Hong Kong is reinventing itself by hosting 'dim sum raves,' combining traditional dining with electronic music and DJs. The initiative aims to appeal to a younger 'Gen Z' demographic and revitalize the business after pandemic-related downturns. The events feature neon lights and dance floors alongside classic Cantonese dishes like prawn dumplings.

articles.headline: Research explores why social circles shrink as adults age
articles.summary: New research and expert commentary explore the phenomenon of shrinking social circles in adulthood. Factors such as changing life priorities, relocation, and the natural fading of casual acquaintances contribute to smaller friend groups. Experts suggest that maintaining adult friendships requires intentional effort, emphasizing that quality of connections often becomes more important.

articles.headline: Exploring the cultural significance of traditional roofs in Indian cooking
articles.summary: Traditional roofs in Indian rural architecture serve as more than just shelter; they are integral to food preservation and cooking. In regions like Uttarakhand, items like herbs and seeds are suspended from kitchen rafters to be cured by wood smoke. Similarly, in other parts of India, flat roofs are used for sun-drying spices, highlighting the connection between architecture and cuisine.

articles.headline: Mumbai Indians' star-studded lineup fails to find cohesion in IPL
articles.summary: Despite having a roster of global cricket superstars like Hardik Pandya, Rohit Sharma, and Suryakumar Yadav, the Mumbai Indians are struggling to perform as a cohesive unit in the current IPL season. Analysts point to a lack of team chemistry and internal friction as key reasons for their poor performance. Individual talent alone is insufficient without a unified team spirit.

articles.headline: Venus Williams continues her professional tennis career at age 45
articles.summary: At 45, tennis legend Venus Williams continues to compete on the professional circuit, defying expectations of retirement. Despite a series of recent losses, Williams remains committed to the sport, stating her love for the game and her desire to continue playing. Her endurance and passion for tennis serve as an inspiration to fans and athletes, showcasing her remarkable longevity.

articles.headline: Armand Duplantis wins Shanghai Diamond League but misses world record
articles.summary: World record holder Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis won the pole vault event at the Shanghai Diamond League but failed to set a new world record. Duplantis cleared 6.12m to secure the victory but was unable to surpass his own record of 6.24m in subsequent attempts. Despite missing the record, his performance confirms his status as the dominant force in the sport.

articles.headline: Aronimink Golf Club hosts challenging PGA Championship
articles.summary: The Aronimink Golf Club is hosting the PGA Championship, presenting a significant challenge to the world's top golfers. The course's difficult layout and fast greens have tested the field, with only a few players managing to stay under par. Analysts note that the course's demanding setup has delivered a tournament worthy of a major championship as leaders compete for the title.

articles.headline: Union Minister Bandi Sanjay’s son arrested in Telangana POCSO case
articles.summary: Bandi Sai Bhageerath, son of MoS Home Bandi Sanjay Kumar, surrendered and was arrested by Cyberabad police in a POCSO case involving the alleged sexual assault of a 17-year-old girl. The arrest followed a week-long search and the High Court's refusal to grant interim protection. A verdict on his anticipatory bail petition is reserved for May 21. His father says all must abide by the law.

articles.headline: CBI arrests Pune teacher as source of NEET-UG paper leak
articles.summary: The CBI arrested Manisha Gurunath Mandhare, a Pune-based botany teacher and NTA paper-setting panel member, for allegedly leaking NEET-UG 2026 questions. Mandhare reportedly shared Botany and Zoology questions during coaching classes held at her home in April. This marks the ninth arrest in the investigation and the second involving an NTA expert in an organized paper-leak network.

articles.headline: Andhra CM announces cash incentives for families having more children
articles.summary: Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu announced a new population policy offering ₹30,000 for a third child and ₹40,000 for a fourth to counter the state’s declining fertility rate of 1.5. The plan includes ₹1,000 monthly nutritional support for five years and free education. Naidu described human resources as "real wealth" and warned that a shrinking working-age population slows economic growth.

articles.headline: PM Modi oversees Tata-ASML semiconductor deal during Netherlands visit
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Dutch PM Rob Jetten in The Hague to strengthen ties as Tata Electronics signed a deal with Dutch tech giant ASML. ASML will provide lithography tools, advanced technology, and training for India's first semiconductor fabrication plant in Dholera, Gujarat. Additionally, the Netherlands returned an 11th-century Chola dynasty copper plate to India.

articles.headline: NCB seizes 227kg of ‘Jihadi drug’ Captagon in major international bust
articles.summary: In India's first major bust of the stimulant Captagon, the Narcotics Control Bureau seized 227.7kg worth ₹182 crore in Delhi and Mundra. Linked to terrorist financing, the "jihadi drug" was hidden in sheep wool and machinery arriving from Iran, destined for Saudi Arabia. A Syrian national was arrested. Home Minister Amit Shah hailed the operation as a major step toward a drug-free India.

articles.headline: CBSE mandates three-language study for Classes 9 and 10
articles.summary: Starting July 1, the CBSE has made the study of three languages compulsory for Classes 9 and 10, with at least two being native Indian languages. Aligned with the National Education Policy 2020, foreign languages can only be taken as a third or fourth option. Assessments for the third language will be school-based, and no student will be barred from Board exams due to it.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay allocates portfolios; Sengottaiyan becomes Finance Minister
articles.summary: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay allocated portfolios to his new TVK government, retaining control over Home, Police, and Municipal Administration. Senior leader K.A. Sengottaiyan was appointed Finance Minister, S. Keerthana took Industries, and K.G. Arunraj was assigned Health. The nine-minister cabinet aims to focus on rural development, public health, and social welfare programs.

articles.headline: India restricts silver imports after surge in monthly volumes
articles.summary: The Union government has moved silver from the ‘free’ to ‘restricted’ category for imports to preserve foreign exchange reserves. The move follows a 157.16% surge in imports during April and a recent hike in import duties on precious metals to 15%. Importers now require specific government authorization as the state aims to curb domestic demand amid rising global oil prices.

articles.headline: CJI clarifies 'parasite' remark was aimed at fake degree holders
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant clarified that his recent oral observations regarding "cockroaches" and "parasites" were directed at individuals using fake degrees to enter noble professions like law and media, not the nation's youth. Expressing pain over being misquoted, the CJI emphasized his respect for Indian youth while warning that dubious credentials pose a threat to professions.

articles.headline: Hindus offer prayers at Bhojshala complex following High Court verdict
articles.summary: Hindu devotees performed Aarti and offered prayers at the Bhojshala complex in Madhya Pradesh after the High Court declared the site a temple dedicated to Goddess Vagdevi. The court quashed a 2003 ASI order that permitted Friday prayers for Muslims, ruling the site is a Saraswati temple. While the ASI granted Hindus unrestricted access, the mosque committee plans to appeal the verdict.

articles.headline: SP leader Azam Khan sentenced to two years jail for 2019 remarks
articles.summary: A special court in Rampur, Uttar Pradesh, sentenced Samajwadi Party leader Azam Khan to two years of imprisonment for making objectionable remarks against government officials and the district magistrate during the 2019 Lok Sabha campaign. Khan, currently lodged in Rampur jail, appeared via video-conferencing for the sentencing, which involved a violation of the Model Code of Conduct.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi demands Education Minister's resignation over NEET paper leak
articles.summary: Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge demanded the immediate resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET-UG paper leak. Gandhi alleged a "money-making racket" involving a nexus between the BJP, RSS, and university faculty. The BJP countered by accusing Gandhi of playing "cheap politics" with the future of 2.2 million students.

articles.headline: Delhi court grants Robert Vadra bail in land deal money laundering case
articles.summary: A Delhi court granted bail to businessman Robert Vadra and co-accused Kewal Singh Virk in a money laundering case linked to a 2008 Gurugram land transaction. The judge observed that detention was unnecessary as Vadra had cooperated with the Enforcement Directorate, which had not arrested him during the probe. Both were directed to furnish bail bonds of ₹50,000 each.

articles.headline: Israel kills Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza strike
articles.summary: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the head of Hamas’ military wing and a key architect of the October 7 attacks. As the most senior Hamas military official killed since the October ceasefire, Al-Haddad had reportedly assumed leadership after Yahya Sinwar's death. The Israeli military stated he used hostages as human shields. His funeral was held in Gaza City.

articles.headline: Vladimir Putin to visit China for strategic partnership talks with Xi
articles.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin is scheduled to visit Beijing on May 19 for a two-day meeting with President Xi Jinping. The leaders will discuss strengthening their "comprehensive partnership," regional security, and economic trade, while exchanging views on the Ukraine-Russia conflict. The trip follows a visit by US President Donald Trump to China that failed to resolve global tensions.

articles.headline: Manchester City wins eighth FA Cup title with victory over Chelsea
articles.summary: Manchester City secured their eighth FA Cup trophy and second silverware of the season with a 1-0 victory over Chelsea at Wembley. Antoine Semenyo scored the decisive goal with an audacious back-heel flick in the second half. Despite a battling defensive display and a rejected penalty claim, Chelsea ended their season without a trophy, while City continued their dominance under Pep Guardiola.

articles.headline: KKR defeat Gujarat Titans to stay alive in IPL playoff race
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders secured a 29-run victory over Gujarat Titans at Eden Gardens to remain in the playoff race. Powered by Finn Allen's 93 off 35 balls and an unbeaten 108-run stand between Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Cameron Green, KKR posted a season-high 247/2. Despite Shubman Gill’s 85 and poor fielding by GT, Sunil Narine’s bowling restricted the Titans to 218/4.

articles.headline: Satwik-Chirag reach Thailand Open final with hard-fought semifinal win
articles.summary: Top seeds Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty reached their first final of the year at the Thailand Open after an 82-minute semifinal battle. The world No. 4 Indian duo defeated Malaysia's Goh Sze Fei and Nur Izzuddin 19-21, 22-20, 21-16. They will face Indonesia's Leo Rolly Carnando and Daniel Marthin for the title as they seek to end their trophy drought and regain their top form.

articles.headline: TCS plans industry-specific AI systems to drive future growth
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) aims to build a unified five-layer AI operating system to provide industry-specific solutions and modernize software stacks. Chairman N. Chandrasekaran announced the firm generated $2.3 billion from AI services in fiscal 2026. TCS plans to establish India’s first high-density AI data centre and adopt a "Human+AI" model to manage global business operations.

articles.headline: Aditya Birla Group to infuse ₹4,730 crore into Vodafone Idea
articles.summary: The Aditya Birla Group approved an equity infusion of ₹4,730 crore into Vodafone Idea through preferential shares and convertible warrants to support its capital needs. While Vi’s fourth-quarter loss narrowed to ₹5,521 crore and ARPU rose to ₹174, the company still faces subscriber churn. The investment aims to improve financial health and network capacity against rivals Jio and Airtel.

articles.headline: Plan to merge PFC and REC moves for Presidential approval
articles.summary: The proposal to merge state-run Power Finance Corporation (PFC) and REC Ltd has been forwarded for Presidential approval, aiming to create a massive power sector lending entity with a combined book of over ₹6 lakh crore. First announced in the FY27 Budget, the merger will see REC's assets and liabilities transferred to PFC. The move is intended to scale up public sector financing efficiency.

articles.headline: India rejects 'illegally constituted' Court of Arbitration award on Indus Treaty
articles.summary: India has categorically rejected an award by the Court of Arbitration at The Hague regarding the Kishanganga and Ratle hydroelectric projects, calling the tribunal "illegally constituted" and its decisions "null and void." New Delhi maintains that technical water storage disputes must be resolved through a Neutral Expert as defined in the 1960 Indus Waters Treaty rather than an arbitral panel.

articles.headline: Centre approves 1,122 additional medical seats for Karnataka government colleges
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission and Union Health Ministry have approved 1,122 additional medical seats in Karnataka’s government colleges for the 2026-27 academic year. The expansion includes 550 undergraduate and 572 postgraduate seats. To support the increase, the Centre sanctioned ₹1,090 crore for infrastructure development, bringing the state’s total undergraduate capacity to 14,495 seats.

articles.headline: Tata Electronics and ASML partner for semiconductor fabrication in Gujarat
articles.summary: Tata Electronics and Dutch firm ASML have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to support the establishment of India's first 300mm wafer fab in Dholera, Gujarat. ASML will provide advanced lithography tools and expertise to help build a resilient domestic semiconductor ecosystem. The collaboration aims to reduce India's reliance on foreign technology for critical semiconductor components.

articles.headline: PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi to attend ICC meeting virtually
articles.summary: Pakistan Cricket Board Chairman Mohsin Naqvi will attend the upcoming ICC Board meeting on May 31 via video conference instead of in person. The decision comes after Naqvi was reportedly not invited to the IPL final in Ahmedabad. The meeting, rescheduled from Doha to a virtual format due to the West Asia crisis, will cover administrative matters and the future international cricket calendar.

articles.headline: Robert Lewandowski to leave FC Barcelona at end of season
articles.summary: Polish striker Robert Lewandowski has announced he will leave FC Barcelona at the end of the current season after three years at the club. Having won a La Liga title, Lewandowski stated his mission is complete and he is ready for a new challenge. Rumors suggest the veteran forward may be considering offers from clubs in Saudi Arabia or Major League Soccer for the next stage of his career.

articles.headline: Islamic State second-in-command killed in joint US-Nigeria military operation
articles.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the worldwide second-in-command of the Islamic State, was killed during a joint military operation by US and Nigerian forces in West Africa. Described as a key coordinator for IS operations, Al-Minuki’s death in northern Nigeria is viewed as a significant blow to the extremist group’s global organizational hierarchy and its efforts to expand influence in the region.

articles.headline: Indian football fans prepare for 2026 FIFA World Cup travel surge
articles.summary: Indian football fans are already planning trips to Canada, Mexico, and the United States for the 2026 FIFA World Cup despite high airfares and a depreciating rupee. Travel experts report a surge in demand for customized itineraries as fans hope to witness icons Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo in what many believe will be their final appearance in the tournament’s historic North American edition.
